# COMP 110 Data Analysis Project 
*By Reese Bunner*

## My Idea
The course should slow down during more difficult topics because students may struggle to keep up with the pace.

## Analysis
I analyzed survey data from COMP110 students to understand how they feel about the pace of the course. I used Python functions to count how many students selected each rating for pace.

## Visualization 1
<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="/comp110-project/static/imgs/pace.png" width="500">

*Figure 1: Distribution of student ratings for course pace.*

## Explanation
Most students selected values around 4 and 5, which suggests the pace is generally moderate but slightly fast. There are also several higher ratings (6 and 7), indicating that some students feel the course moves too quickly. Very few students selected low values, meaning almost no students think the course is too slow.

## Additional Insight
I also looked at the distribution of students by year. Most responses came from underclassmen (Class of 2027 and 2028), which means the data may reflect newer students more than experienced ones.

## Conclusion
Based on this analysis, slowing down during more difficult topics could improve student understanding. While this may reduce the amount of content covered, it would likely help students stay engaged and avoid falling behind.
